question.text: A stockbroker provides Margin Trading Facility. At any point in time, what is the maximum total indebtedness permitted for the purpose of margin trading in relation to its net worth?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the SEBI circular on Comprehensive Review of Margin Trading Facility, at any point in time, the total indebtedness of a stockbroker for the purpose of margin trading shall not exceed 5 times its net worth. **The Trap:** 10 times is tempting as students may confuse this with exposure limits in other contexts. The actual leverage cap is 5 times net worth. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compare with the underwriting obligation limit where total underwriting obligations under all agreements should not exceed 20 times the net worth of the stockbroker acting as an underwriter. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Track indebtedness ratios daily to ensure the 5x cap is never breached. Sudden market movements can push this ratio beyond limits if not monitored.
